mnemonics: MAR is the Address Register: Think 'Memory Address to Read'. It tells the memory where to go. MDR is the Data Register: Think 'Memory Data Received'. It holds what was found there.
keyPoints: The CPU places the desired memory address into the Memory Address Register (MAR).
The CPU issues a 'read' command to the memory unit.
After the access time delay, the fetched word from memory is loaded into the Memory Data Register (MDR) for the CPU to use.
commonMistakes: Assuming the data is sent directly to a CPU register (like R1); it always goes to the MDR first.
Forgetting that the address bus width determines the maximum addressable memory (e.g., 32 bits = 4 GB).
Ignoring the role of the memory controller in managing the read operation and timing.
